content_markdown:
A battery is connected in series with a resistor $R$. The battery transfers $2\,000\text{ C}$ of charge completely round the circuit. During this process, $2\,500\text{ J}$ of energy is dissipated in the resistor $R$ and $1\,500\text{ J}$ is expended in the battery.

The e.m.f. of the battery is

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Electromotive force (e.m.f.) is defined as the total energy supplied by the source per unit charge that passes through it. To find the e.m.f., we must sum the total energy expended in the entire circuit (both in the external resistor and the internal resistance of the battery) and divide by the total charge. Total energy $W = 2\,500\text{ J} + 1\,500\text{ J} = 4\,000\text{ J}$. The charge $Q = 2\,000\text{ C}$. Therefore, $\text{e.m.f.} = \frac{W}{Q} = \frac{4\,000}{2\,000} = 2.00\text{ V}$.
